commit.message:
chore(deps): bump react-dom and @types/react-dom (#37)

* chore(deps): bump react-dom and @types/react-dom

Bumps [react-dom](https://github.com/facebook/react/tree/HEAD/packages/react-dom) and [@types/react-dom](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/tree/HEAD/types/react-dom). These dependencies needed to be updated together.

Updates `react-dom` from 18.3.1 to 19.2.5
- [Release notes](https://github.com/facebook/react/releases)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/facebook/react/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
- [Commits](https://github.com/facebook/react/commits/v19.2.5/packages/react-dom)

Updates `@types/react-dom` from 18.3.7 to 19.2.3
- [Release notes](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/releases)
- [Commits](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/commits/HEAD/types/react-dom)

---
updated-dependencies:
- dependency-name: react-dom
  dependency-version: 19.2.5
  dependency-type: direct:production
  update-type: version-update:semver-major
- dependency-name: "@types/react-dom"
  dependency-version: 19.2.3
  dependency-type: direct:development
  update-type: version-update:semver-major
...

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>

* chore(deps): upgrade react to v19 to match react-dom

- Updated react from ^18.2.0 to ^19.2.5 to match react-dom upgrade
- Updated @types/react from ^18 to ^19 to match @types/react-dom
- Resolved peer dependency conflict causing build failures
- Verified successful build with npm run build

Co-authored-by: Amanda Nelson <jobs@amanda-nelson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>
Co-authored-by: dependabot[bot] <49699333+dependabot[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Amanda Nelson <jobs@amanda-nelson.com>

feat: auto-scale, zoom, sidebar de patrocinadores, remoção do botão + e switch (#1)

* Add AGENTS.md with Cursor Cloud development instructions

Co-authored-by: Jailson Pacagnan Santana <jailsonpacagnans@hotmail.com>

* feat: auto-scale cells, zoom controls, sponsor sidebar, remove + button

- Auto-scale: font and cell sizes adjust proportionally based on row count
  so all numbers fit on screen without scroll even with 75 numbers
- Zoom controls: magnifying glass +/- buttons for manual size adjustment
- Sponsor sidebar: auto-detects images from imagens/ folder (any format),
  rotates randomly every 10 seconds with 'Patrocinadores' heading
- Commented out '+' button; rows are added automatically via insert()
- Fixed plus() to add rows with proper column classes (s0-s4)
- Vendored Bootstrap 5.1.3 bundle locally for offline operation
- Clean resets table to 5 rows and clears all content

Co-authored-by: Jailson Pacagnan Santana <jailsonpacagnans@hotmail.com>

* remove: switch de modo aleatório/sequencial

Co-authored-by: Jailson Pacagnan Santana <jailsonpacagnans@hotmail.com>

- PV01: Ông Nguyễn Minh Trí - Trưởng phòng Pháp chế, Fintech
- PV02: Bà Trần Thị Bích Ngọc - Giám đốc Pháp lý, doanh nghiệp sản xuất

- 7 nhóm node chính, 35 node con với định nghĩa chi tiết

fix: improve game tile labels on /games page (#154)

- Remove meaningless player-count badge ("1" on every tile)
- Prefix difficulty badge with "AI Difficulty:" for context
- Replace "Coming Soon" with "No AI Yet" (warning badge) for the five
  playable games that don't yet have a trained AI integrated
- Pong retains "Coming Soon" as the game itself is not yet playable
- Remove "1 Player" from all tags arrays

DB update for the games table is documented in features/per-game-bugs/spec.md.

Durable ingest jobs: per-job worker defaults (extraction, store, remediation) (#150)

* feat(ingest-jobs): per-job worker defaults for durable batch UI

- Add ingestion_jobs.worker_defaults jsonb (migration 0014) and Drizzle column.
- Sanitize API payload; merge into each child ingest batch_overrides when the
  job ticks (extraction/store concurrency, remediation caps, watchdog JSON, etc.).
- Durable jobs page: collapsible worker defaults + localStorage persistence.
- Restore batch_overrides env mapping for passage/claim concurrency and watchdog
  tuning in ingestRuns (used by jobs + single-run paths).

spec: per-game bugs (#96-99), cross-game conventions, ADR (#152)

* spec: per-game bug fixes (issues #96-#99)

Game-specific bugs not covered by UX standardization:
- Connect4: column click zone + hover preview
- Dots & Boxes: box fill icons, button alignment
- Chess: board orientation, piece images, AN notation, FEN storage, captures, scrolling
- Checkers: forced capture visual, turn indicator, AI timing delay
Depends on ux-game-standardization being merged first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* spec: add games page label bugs to per-game-bugs

Documents the three display issues on /games: missing difficulty context,
useless player count badge, and "Coming Soon" applied to playable games.
Notes that frontend changes are already implemented; DB update SQL included.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* spec: fix DB SQL to use array_remove/array_append (tags is text[])

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* spec: document PR checkout, PGN regen, global event pacing, optimistic UX

Record cross-game conventions in per-game-bugs: FEN plus SAN move_list with
PGN regenerated on demand; single GAME_SERVER_MIN_EVENT_INTERVAL_MS for all
SSE games; optimistic animations vs authoritative rejection handling;
explicit not-doing for premove and deferred checkers path UX. CONTRIBUTING
adds gh pr checkout guidance so review edits land on the PR branch.

* docs: PR body maintenance, ADR policy, per-game-bugs ADR

CONTRIBUTING: require PR title/body updates during review; document ADR
workflow and link ADRs from PR descriptions. CLAUDE.md: align feature
steps with CONTRIBUTING. Add features/per-game-bugs/adr.md for cross-game
move storage, global SSE pacing, and optimistic UI; link from spec.

* docs: Cursor auth failures - ask user for gh/gcloud on Windows

Add CONTRIBUTING §1e: on GitHub/GCP permission errors, direct the user to
complete gh auth login and gcloud auth flows on their machine (Credential
Manager), never tokens in chat; note gh pr edit vs REST workaround and
cloud-only agent caveat. CLAUDE.md points to §1e.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

Kitchen-first UI: bottom nav, PRINT FAB, collapsed More menu (#2)

* feat: complete Cheftag Solutions app

- Next.js 15 (App Router) + React 19 + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui v4
- Dashboard with 6 KPI cards, 3 pie charts, bar chart, recent activity
- Labels page with grid view, filters, bulk actions, quick print
- Products/Employees/Groups pages with TanStack Table v8
- Devices page with card layout and test print
- Reports page with full table + CSV/PDF export
- Preservation Modes and Settings management pages
- Printing page with queue and device selection
- Zustand store with localStorage persistence
- i18n translation system (English, ready for pt-BR)
- Purple/Gold color scheme, dark mode support
- Mobile bottom navigation, responsive design
- Keyboard shortcuts (N = new label, / = focus search)

Co-authored-by: JonnySchipper <JonnySchipper@users.noreply.github.com>

- Collapse navigation to Home, Labels, Print, and More (reports + admin)
- Add desktop sidebar mirroring the same sections from xl breakpoint
- Add global PRINT and New label FABs with print quick sheet and queue helpers
- Enlarge touch targets, KPI cards, label cards, and status badges for tablets
- Auto-queue new labels and highlight print action after create
- Update README and AGENTS for the new navigation model

Co-authored-by: JonnySchipper <JonnySchipper@users.noreply.github.com>

spec: test audit and /bug regression tool (#153)

* spec: test audit and /bug regression tool

Audits coverage gaps (4 games missing E2E, leaderboard, chess FEN),
identifies structural issues (two parallel API test suites, JS/TS mismatch,
seed password inconsistency), adds leaderboard + chess FEN tests, adds
E2E for all 5 games, and introduces a /bug slash command for creating
failing regression tests before fixes are written.

definitions (five shipped; Pong deferred), link to test-coverage-overhaul
for full-site pyramid goals, smoke routes in scope, and CONTRIBUTING
cross-reference for process text landing in PR #159.

docs(docker): esclarecer seed e suportar RUN_SEED no Compose

O seed nunca corre no build; por defeito também não na subida (evita sync PJE a cada restart). RUN_SEED=true no .env executa npm run seed antes do Nest. README: nota sobre Postgres fora do bloco bash.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

docs(env): NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL e API_URL_INTERNAL

Documenta URL da API no browser e variável opcional para server-side no Docker.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

chore(docker): API_URL_INTERNAL para o frontend no Compose

Aponta para o serviço backend na rede interna, respeitando BACKEND_PORT.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

fix(frontend): base URL da API no servidor vs browser

Server Components e NextAuth usam API_URL_INTERNAL no Docker; o cliente mantém NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL (localhost).

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

fix(backend): usar bcryptjs em vez de bcrypt nativo

Evita SIGSEGV no Alpine/musl; hashes existentes continuam compatíveis no compare.

Core architecture changes:
- New plan.py: Plan data model with id, status, persistence in .meta/plans/
- chat() no longer intercepts write tools; model uses observation + submit_plan
- New execute_plan(): separate LLM call to faithfully implement approved plans
- System verifies model's change_type classification (policy.py)
- Incremental plans auto-execute; structural plans require user approval
- CLI and gateway updated for new plan-based approval flow
- Prompts rewritten: exploration/planning vs execution separation
- Legacy list[dict] backward compatibility preserved

  legacy migration, and classify_change_type
- Update CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md for new architecture

All 583 tests pass.

fix: resolve broken ticket submission forms (#18)

Two issues fixed:

1. submit-ticket.mdx - Existing Ticket Lookup form:
   - Removed inline POST form with hardcoded stale CSRF authenticity_token
   - Rails generates per-session CSRF tokens, so a static token baked into
     the MDX page will never validate, causing submissions to silently fail
   - Replaced with a direct link to the Syncro check_ins portal where the
     form renders natively with a valid session-bound token

2. submit-ticket.mdx - New Service Ticket form:
   - Removed noValidate attribute so browser enforces required email field
   - Added target=_blank so the form submits to Syncro in a new tab,
     preventing Mintlify SPA routing from intercepting the submission

3. client-login.mdx - Client Portal Login form:
   - Updated form action from /my_profile/do_login to /portal/do_login
     (the old URL 302-redirects, which drops POST body in browsers)
   - Added target=_blank for reliable cross-origin form submission

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

feat: add inspectAgent child inspection (#69)

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Max Weinbach <mweinbach@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(neon): use alias ir in idle watchdog candidate SQL (#149)

FROM ingest_runs ir cannot reference unqualified ingest_runs columns;
Drizzle's idleStallWhereSql expanded to "ingest_runs"."last_output_at"
which fails in PostgreSQL and broke durable jobs UI refresh.

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Adam Boon <adamboon1984-arch@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve admin setup and login redirect loop (#42)

Three bugs combined to prevent a user from staying logged in after
creating the admin account or signing in:

1. SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE defaulted to True in config.py.
   Browsers silently discard Secure cookies over plain HTTP, so the
   session cookie was never stored after login/setup, making every
   subsequent API call return 401.  Changed default to False to match
   the docker-compose.yml default and support the majority of
   self-hosted (LAN/HTTP) deployments out of the box.  Operators
   behind HTTPS must opt in with SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=true.

2. The global Axios 401 interceptor only exempted /login.
   Any 401 received while the browser was on /setup, /forgot-password,
   or /reset-password triggered a hard redirect to /login.  The initial
   unauthenticated GET /auth/me probe (expected to 401) would therefore
   navigate away from the setup wizard.  Added those public paths to
   the exemption list.

3. Race condition between the initial /auth/me probe and a successful
   login.  If the probe's 401 arrived after navigate('/') was called,
   the interceptor saw a non-login path and bounced the user back.
   Fixed by holding an AbortController ref for the probe in
   AuthProvider and aborting it inside login() before the credentials
   POST resolves.  Cancelled requests are also now silently ignored by
   the interceptor (axios.isCancel check).

Updated test_config.py to assert the new False default.

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: titatom <titatom@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rescope Apple surrogates/blocking scripts migration to retain native-side resolver (#2583)

* S3-1: Raw resource observer with surrogateInjectionEnabled gate

Replace rich trackerDetected with raw resourceObserved signal:
- Emit {url, resourceType, potentiallyBlocked, pageUrl} only
- No tracker/entity/reason/prevalence classification from JS
- Native TrackerResolver is sole classification authority

surrogateInjectionEnabled gate:
- When false (shadow phase): no surrogate injection, no surrogateInjected
- _loadSurrogate throws if called with gate off (fail-fast)
- When true (post-cutover): surrogate injection active

surrogateInjected schema minimized:
- {url, pageUrl, surrogateName} — no rich classification fields
- Native maps to full DetectedRequest via TrackerResolver

tracker-resolver.js retained for surrogate matching against filtered TDS.

* Add generated type fixes

* Trigger hosted build

* Update integration tests to use resourceObserved instead of trackerDetected

The tracker-protection feature now sends 'resourceObserved' (raw observation
data: url, resourceType, potentiallyBlocked, pageUrl) instead of the classified
'trackerDetected' message. The 'surrogateInjected' schema was also minimized
to {url, pageUrl, surrogateName}.

Update all integration test assertions to match the new message contract.

* Fix integration test configs and fetch assertions

- Add surrogateInjectionEnabled: true to configs that back surrogate
  tests (tracker-protection.json, ctl-enabled.json, real-surrogates.json).
  Without this, the feature gates off surrogate injection and
  surrogateInjected is never emitted, causing 5s timeouts.

- Fix fetch interception tests to expect potentiallyBlocked: false.
  The fetch wrapper always reports false since it uses _reportResource
  (observation-only), not _checkAndBlock. Native does classification.

* Fix DOM-appended img potentiallyBlocked assertion

MutationObserver img path uses _reportResource with potentiallyBlocked=false
(observation-only, no classification). Test incorrectly expected true.

All 24 tracker-protection integration tests now pass locally.

* Add trackerProtection to platform-specific features to fix protections-off reporting

* Address review: use getFeatureSettingEnabled, restore comments, remove unused destroy()

- Use getFeatureSettingEnabled for blocking and surrogateInjection settings
  (standard C-S-S pattern supporting state objects with enabled/disabled/internal/preview)
- Restore 2 useful comments: top-level URL context and trackerData origin
- Remove unused destroy() method (ContentFeature has no destroy lifecycle)
- Update all test config JSONs to use state object format

* Auto-format: prettier + stylelint fix

* refactor: use getFeatureSettingEnabled for ctlEnabled setting

Change ctlEnabled from bare boolean check (getFeatureSetting !== false) to
getFeatureSettingEnabled('ctlEnabled', 'enabled') matching the pattern used
by blocking and surrogateInjection settings.

Update all integration test config JSON files to use the { state: 'enabled' }
/ { state: 'disabled' } shape instead of bare true/false.

Co-authored-by: Jonathan Kingston <jonathanKingston@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: restore dropped JSDoc comments on tracker-protection methods

Restore method-level JSDoc comments that were removed during the refactor:
- _setupInterception: resource interception setup
- _processAddedNode: DOM node processing
- _scanExistingScripts: early DOMContentLoaded scan
- _processPageOnLoad: full page load scan
- _loadSurrogate: surrogate loading with parameter descriptions

Co-authored-by: Jonathan Kingston <jonathanKingston@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename ctlEnabled setting key to ctl for consistency

Aligns with blocking and surrogateInjection key naming convention.

* Eliminate config generator: read settings from bundledConfig directly

TrackerProtection now reads blocking, CTL, and allowlist state directly
from the privacy config features (contentBlocking, clickToLoad,
trackerAllowlist) instead of from reshuffled trackerProtection.settings.

Introduces selfGatingFeatures concept: features that bypass exception-based
disabling in computeEnabledFeatures and handle their own exceptions internally.
This allows trackerProtection to stay active on excepted domains for
dashboard reporting while adjusting behavior (potentiallyBlocked: false).

- Add selfGatingFeatures list and isSelfGatingFeature to utils.js
- Refactor tracker-protection.js init to read from bundledConfig
- Remove unprotected domain params/methods from TrackerResolver
- Restructure test configs to match real remote config shape
- Document selfGatingFeatures in features-guide.md

* Return false instead of throwing when surrogateInjection is disabled in _loadSurrogate

* Fix failing C-S-S tests: update TrackerResolver unit tests and config schemas

- Cast _isStateEnabled args to FeatureState type (TS2345)
- Remove obsolete unprotectedDomains/userUnprotectedDomains params from
  TrackerResolver constructor calls in unit and reference tests
- Remove isUnprotectedDomain describe block testing removed methods
- Add required 'exceptions' field to trackerAllowlist in test configs

* Fix isUnprotectedDomain for single-label hostnames (e.g. localhost)

The loop condition domainParts.length > 1 skipped single-label hostnames,
so 'localhost' was never matched against the exceptions list. Changed to
>= 1 to handle this case.

* Guard allowlist parsing against malformed config entries

* Remove unused getEntityAffiliation from TrackerResolver

* Remove stale FAIL FAST else block from surrogate injection

* chore: remove unused trackerDetected schema and regenerate types

Delete trackerDetected.notify.json (no callers remain after refactor)
and regenerate tracker-protection.ts to drop the dead
TrackerDetectedNotification type from the notifications union.

Co-authored-by: Jonathan Kingston <jonathanKingston@users.noreply.github.com>

* CTL parity: suppress potentiallyBlocked for CTL-disabled block-ctl-* rules

When CTL is disabled and a matched rule action starts with `block-ctl-`,
do not report potentiallyBlocked: true. This prevents the native mapper
from misclassifying CTL-only requests as blocked when CTL is inactive.

Updated integration tests to expect potentiallyBlocked: false for
CTL-disabled scenarios and added a control test confirming non-CTL rules
remain potentiallyBlocked: true.

Made-with: Cursor

* Lint

* Use captured Object.entries/fromEntries in tracker-protection allowlist parsing

Page-world code must use captured globals to prevent hostile pages from
influencing allowlist extraction via monkeypatched Object methods.

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Jonathan Kingston <jonathanKingston@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Jonathan Kingston <jkingston@duckduckgo.com>

commit.message:
Improve ingest idle watchdog, worker heartbeats, and phase UX (#148)

- Add worker_heartbeat_at column and bump it from telemetry heartbeats
  so long model calls do not inflate log-driven idle or mask dead workers.
- Watchdog uses max(last_output_at, worker_heartbeat_at) and optional
  per-phase thresholds (INGEST_WATCHDOG_PHASE_IDLE_JSON) or baseline
  multiplier from the last INGEST_TIMING line.
- Default telemetry heartbeat to 45s when orchestration run id is set.
- Pipeline: remediation stage in ordered stages and admin UI; progress
  logs during remediation; higher default extraction concurrency and
  lower default remediation claim cap.
- Status API exposes workerHeartbeatAt and combined lastActivityAt.

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Adam Boon <adamboon1984-arch@users.noreply.github.com>

Backend (.NET 8 Web API):
- Task CRUD endpoints (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH status)
- SQLite database with EF Core
- TaskItem model with priority, status, due dates, reminders, tags
- Filtering by status/priority, search, and sorting

Frontend (React + TypeScript + Vite):

Add Cursor rules, AGENTS.md, and consolidate contributing docs (#158)

* Add Cursor rules, AGENTS.md, and consolidate docs in CONTRIBUTING.md

- Add .cursor/rules for always-on, backend, frontend, specs/ADRs, tests/CI, and GitHub workflow globs
- Add AGENTS.md for short agent defaults pointing to CONTRIBUTING
- Merge former CLAUDE.md workflow into CONTRIBUTING (spec-first, ADR bar, branch/CI, pip-tools, test tiers)
- Replace CLAUDE.md with a deprecation pointer; update README and feature spec references

* Use python3 in npm test scripts for environments without python shim

* Cursor rules: staff-engineer tone, pre-push test gate, PR watch + auto-merge

- Always rule: lead with solution; audience tech lead/staff; npm run test:fast before push; no --no-verify unless user allows; gh pr checks --watch after create; gh pr merge --auto --squash by default
- branch-pr-workflow.mdc: align with always rule
- AGENTS.md and CONTRIBUTING.md: same policy; Prettier

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

Upgrade scan_vault_context() to tiered vault map (#41)

Replace the old stats-only vault overview with a navigation-oriented
vault map that gives the LLM enough structure to route queries without
an extra tool call.

Tiered output based on vault size:
- Small (<40 pages): full page index with summaries under each hub
- Medium (40-150): compact index (title + path, no summaries)
- Large (150+): hub structure with truncated member lists

New information included:
- Hub→page membership mapping (pages listed under their hub)
- Unorganized pages listed individually (not just a count)
- Journal entry count and date range
- Sources subdirectories with sample filenames (up to 3 per dir)

Also fixes:
- Stale tool names in docstring (list_page_summaries, search_vault,
  find_existing_page → list_pages, search, survey_topic)
- Stale empty-vault detection in agent.py (page titles (0) → Total: 0 pages)
- Footer hint now references all four navigation tools

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: forjiuzhou <forjiuzhou@users.noreply.github.com>

test: validate lint auto-fix workflow_dispatch status reporting (#4944)

* fix: report commit statuses on workflow_dispatch so PR checks pass

When the lint auto-fix pushes a new commit and dispatches a
workflow_dispatch rerun, the resulting check runs don't satisfy
PR required status checks (GitHub doesn't associate workflow_dispatch
check runs with the PR's check suite).

Add commit status reporting steps for both unit and lint jobs that
post statuses via the GitHub Statuses API when triggered by
workflow_dispatch. This makes the results visible to branch
protection.

Co-authored-by: Jonathan Kingston <jonathanKingston@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add contents: read to unit job permissions

When job-level permissions are declared, all unmentioned permissions
default to none. The unit job needs contents: read for checkout.

Co-authored-by: Jonathan Kingston <jonathanKingston@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: introduce lint error to validate auto-fix workflow

Missing trailing newline in tab-progress-indicator.ts will trigger
prettier --check failure. The lint job should auto-fix, push, and
dispatch a workflow_dispatch that posts commit statuses.

Co-authored-by: Jonathan Kingston <jonathanKingston@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: auto-fix lint errors

* test: re-introduce lint error for second validation round

Co-authored-by: Jonathan Kingston <jonathanKingston@users.noreply.github.com>

list_pages/fetch_url/capture/ingest/organize/restructure/write_page),
preserve vault write guards, and add coverage for search scopes, title

TOOL_TIERS, organize read-before-write, and capture-based append flows.

Co-authored-by: forjiuzhou <forjiuzhou@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: redesign tool system from 22 file-level tools to 11 knowledge-centric tools

Core changes:
- Observation tools (6): read_page, search, survey_topic, get_backlinks, list_pages, fetch_url
- Action tools (5): capture, ingest, organize, restructure, write_page
- Automated: append_log (auto after plan), add_related_link (auto via progressive disclosure)
- System prompt rewritten for new tool paradigm

fix: 修复 Gian 角色狂暴状态切换和嘴巴显示问题 (#1)

问题1: 狂暴状态的嘴巴贴图在正常状态下也会显示
问题2: 吃3个果子后未触发从正常到狂暴的状态转换
问题3: 缺少狂暴状态5秒后恢复正常的逻辑

根本原因:
- _process() 中通过 scene.has_node('EntitySpawner') 获取 spawner 并连接
  players_spawned 信号，但该信号在 EntitySpawner.init() 时已经发射完毕，
  导致 _on_players_spawned 从未被调用
- _on_players_spawned 未调用意味着:
  a) _hide_mouth_layers 未执行 → 嘴巴一直显示
  b) _try_hook_consumables_container 未执行 → 果子监听未启用

修复方案(参考 gooodgame-luffy 模型的实现):
1. 改用 scene._entity_spawner 属性获取 spawner (与 luffy 一致)
2. 连接 players_spawned 后检查 _players 是否已有数据，有则立即调用
   _on_players_spawned (解决信号已发射的时序问题)
3. 添加 _exit_berserk 函数，通过 Timer 实现5秒后恢复正常状态
4. 预加载正常状态贴图 TEX_HAT_NORMAL/TEX_TAIL_NORMAL 用于恢复

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Zzgj <Zzgj@users.noreply.github.com>
